question: The spinothalamic tract crosses to the contralateral side at the level of:

A 24-year-old underwent myelography to determine the injury to spinal cord, the needle with the contrast was introduced in the subarachnoid space. At which one of the following vertebral levels was the needle introduced:
A 34-year-old man after a road traffic accident came with a fracture of T-7 vertebra. Which one of the following spinal segment will be affected in this case?
A 10-year-old boy developed a viral infection, after which he developed flaccid type of paralysis in his right lower limb with loss of knee jerk reflex. This may be due to damage to:
choices: C. Right dorsal horn cells of the spinal cord
D. Right ventral horn cells of the spinal cord
E. Left ventral horn cell of the spinal cord

question: 30-year-old Rasool received multiple gunshot injuries in the back while resisting an attempted robbery. After initial resuscitation, an MRI was performed to assess the extent of damage to his spinal cord. This showed hemi-section of the spinal cord at the level of the Right T6 vertebra. Three weeks later, the neurological examination of his lower limbs showed unilateral stiffness with brisk deep tendon reflexes, and positive Babinski's sign. With the above mentioned lesion, Rasool is expected to have loss of pain and temperature in:
explanation: In Brown-Séquard syndrome at right T6, the spinothalamic tract (pain/temperature) crosses within 1-2 spinal levels of entry, so the deficit is contralateral (left side) below the lesion level. The left lower limb (E) is the primary answer; the source lists D and E but D (left groin region) is also contralateral and at a level near the lesion.

question: 30-year-old Rasool received multiple gunshot injuries in the back while resisting an attempted robbery. After initial resuscitation, an MRI was performed to assess the extent of damage to his spinal cord. This showed hemi-section of the spinal cord at the level of the Right T6 vertebra. Three weeks later, the neurological examination of his lower limbs showed unilateral stiffness with brisk deep tendon reflexes, and positive Babinski's sign. Which of the following on examination is indicative of a complete hemi-section of the dorsal column?
choices: A. Intact vibration sensation on the right side below T6 spinal segment
B. Loss of conscious proprioception on the right below T6 spinal segment
C. Loss of discriminative touch on the left starting at T8 spinal segment
D. Loss of conscious proprioception on the left below T6 spinal segment
E. Loss of pain and temperature on the right side at T8 spinal segment

question: A 25-year-old man had a head injury resulting in skull fracture from a car accident. He recovers without any clinical sequelae. Which central nervous system cell type is most likely to undergo hypertrophy and hyperplasia as a response to the injury?
explanation: Both astrocytes (gliosis/hypertrophy) and microglia (proliferation/activation) respond to CNS injury. The source accepts both A and C.

question: Calcium ions are important to synaptic transmission for which of the following reasons:
choices: A. They are required for synthesis of neurotransmitter in the cytoplasm
B. Because increase in the extracellular calcium level induces neurotransmitter release
C. They act on the postsynaptic receptor inducing a permeability change in the membrane
D. Because increase in the intracellular calcium level induce neurotransmitter release from the presynaptic terminal
E. Their efflux induces depolarization of the postsynaptic terminal

question: Our finger tips have high special tactile acuity due to which of the following receptor property?
choices: A. Large receptive field and low receptor density
C. Small receptive field and high receptor density
D. Information is carried out by A-beta afferents

question: The resting membrane potential of a cell is due to unequal distribution of charged ions across the membrane. Which of the following is most important in maintaining this unequal distribution?
choices: A. High extracellular concentration of sodium
B. High intracellular concentration of potassium
C. Presence of non-diffusible negatively charged intracellular ions
D. The electrogenic property of Na+/K+ ATPase
E. The membrane is more permeable to certain ions than others

question: While experimenting with a nerve muscle preparation, a medical student adds a solution that caused the nerves to generate action potentials and the muscle to contract. Which explanation below supports the hypothesis of the student that the solution added contained K+ ions?
choices: A. The resting membrane potential moved closer to EK
B. K+ ions diffused out of the neuron triggering an action potential
C. Na+ diffused into the cell to reestablish the resting membrane potential
D. The resting membrane potential moved closer to the ENa
E. The resting membrane potential became less negative triggering an action potential

question: During a neuronal action potential there is a period when the cell cannot be activated by another stimulus. Which choice below explains this period?
choices: A. The voltage-gated Na+ channel is closed
B. The voltage-gated K+ channel is open
C. The EK has yet to be reached
D. The Na+ channel is inactivated
E. The K+ channel is inactivated

question: Match up the correct pairing of a receptor type with its action.
choices: A. Ionotropic; direct opening of an ion channel
B. Ionotropic; more time required to open an ion channel
C. Ionotropic; G-protein activation leads to activation of a second messenger
D. Metabotropic; a second messenger effects that are specific to neuronal communication
E. Metabotropic; rapid and short-lived effects on ion channels

question: A 26-year-old women is found wearing high-heeled shoes, noted marked pain in the left foot for the past 3 months. On physical examination, she has severe pain on palpation of the interdigital space between the second and third toe. There is no visible swelling or erythema of the foot. Motor strength in the lower extremities seem to be normal. What kind of neuropathy is she suffering from?

A 38-year-old woman develops numbness and tingling in her feet followed by an ascending paralysis syndrome that worsens over 7 days. Her examination is remarkable for moderate paresis in all limbs, areflexia, and minimal sensory loss. CSF protein level is increased and there are 3 lymphocytes/ml. She recalls mild gastroenteritis 6 weeks prior to the onset of symptoms. Which of the following infections is most likely to be associated with her neurologic illness?

A 24-year-old woman suffers a deep injury to the forearm. On physical examination there is loss of sensation over fifth digit and the medial half of the fourth digit, and the corresponding part of the palm. Which of the following pathologic changes explains the loss of sensation?

A 50-year-old man has experienced severe headaches, worsening over the past 2 months. He is afebrile. On physical examination papilledema is present, worse on the left. MRI reveals an irregular mass in the left cortical hemisphere with midline shift and generalized brain edema. What is the mechanism responsible in pathogenesis of this edema?
choices: A. Blockage of lymphatics by metastatic tumor
B. Overproduction of cerebrospinal fluid by the tumor
C. Obstruction of aqueduct of Sylvius by the tumor
D. Increased vascular permeability caused by the tumor

question: A virus crosses the blood brain barrier and invades the brain. Which of the following cells first detect and fight the invading virus?

Examination of a 55-year-old male patient was significant for increased tone in the left arm. The resistance was stronger at the beginning of the range of motion. It increased with higher speed of mobilization. Which of the following is applicable to this patient's signs?
Sleep disturbance and loss of appetite are symptoms of depression due to abnormality in which of the following brain areas?
A 30-year-old man feels that his neighbors are able to read his mind. What type of condition is he suffering from?
Which part of the internal capsule transmits auditory fibers?

A 65-year-old hypertensive man presents to the emergency department with sudden onset of weakness on the left side of the body. Neurological examination revealed spastic paralysis involving the left half of the body and the lower part of the left face. There was complete sensory loss on the left side of the body. Babinski's sign was positive on the left side. An MRI was performed. Which one of the following areas is involved in this case?
choices: A. Right anterior limb of the internal capsule
B. Right posterior limb of the internal capsule
D. Right frontal and parietal lobe

question: A 60-year-old man was admitted to the emergency department for sudden onset abnormal movement in his right limbs during daily activities. The symptoms were alleviated during relaxation and completely eliminated during sleep. Neurological examination revealed involuntary proximal ballistic movement affecting both upper and lower limbs on the right side. An MRI performed two days later revealed an infarct involving which one of the following areas?

A 21-year-old male brought to the ER by his family members in a state of excitement, violent attitude, and insomnia for more than 24 hours. Upon examination, he was extremely agitated, sweaty and restless. His vital signs show tachycardia, high blood pressure and mild fever. His brother tells you that he has been very anxious lately because of his final exams next week. His past history is unremarkable. You suspect that he has taken a drug. Which of the following is most likely the drug he has taken?
Which one is a non-psychotic indication of typical antipsychotic drugs?

A 26-year-old female patient with refractory depression was maintained on an antidepressant drug. She reports attacks of palpitation, shortness of breath, and headache whenever she eats cheese. Her blood pressure was also found to be high during these attacks. Which class of antidepressants is she most likely on?

A patient with acquired neurological damage manages to exhibit his teeth symmetrically upon request. However he cannot exhibit a symmetrical smile on the left side of the face after a joke. Which of the following is true for this case?
choices: A. Voluntary facial paresis with damage on the right primary motor cortex
B. Voluntary facial paresis with damage on the right thalamic pathway
C. Emotional facial paresis with damage on the right primary motor cortex
D. Emotional facial paresis with damage on the right thalamic pathway
E. Voluntary facial paresis with damage on the left primary motor cortex

question: Answering the question 'In which country are the pyramids?' correctly requires which memory system?

Mr. John has suffered a stroke in the area shown in the figure. He occasionally disregards the left side of his body. Which one of the following cognitive tests would be advised after reviewing the patient's neuroradiological report?
Which of the following is most common in neonatal meningitis?
Which of the following best applies to the viral agent shown in the microscopy image from the hippocampus?
choices: A. Having specific geographic location (Middle East and Africa)
C. Being more diffuse in winter
D. Transmitted by mosquitoes of genus Anopheles
E. Being preventable by live attenuated vaccine

question: The profile of CSF investigation presented here suggested what type of etiology?

During a neurosurgical procedure, an electrical stimulus applied to a part of the brain resulted in a discrete contralateral movement across a single joint. Which of the following brain areas was likely being stimulated?
The posterior inferior cerebellar artery (PICA) arises from which main stem artery?

A 48-year-old male presented with severe unilateral headache with accompanying nausea and vomiting. After exclusion of other causes, you make a diagnosis of migraine and consider prescribing sumatriptan. However, history of which one of the following diseases would prevent you from prescribing this drug?

A 70-year-old male with new onset Parkinson's disease was prescribed a combination of levodopa and carbidopa. One rationale of adding carbidopa is to minimize the development of adverse effects of levodopa. Which one of the following adverse effects of levodopa would be effectively controlled by carbidopa?
Which one of the following medications used for treatment of meningitis requires therapeutic monitoring of the blood concentration?

An 88-year-old woman with Alzheimer disease dies of congestive heart failure. Examination of the brain at autopsy shows bilateral atrophy of the gyri, particularly in the frontal, parietal and temporal cortex. What additional finding might be expected in the brain of this patient?

A 70-year-old man has had stepwise loss of mental function for the past 10 years. He can no longer care for himself. Physical examination is unremarkable but he cannot remember any of 3 objects after 3 minutes. A chest X-ray shows cardiomegaly. Laboratory studies show a normal serum glucose and increased level of cholesterol and LDL. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 42-year-old man with a long history of productive cough presented to A&E with fever, increasing headache and drowsiness for the past one week. On physical examination his temperature is 38.6 degrees. A head CT scan reveals a solitary 4 cm diameter mass lesion with ring enhancement located in the left parietal lobe. A stereotactic biopsy is performed and a frozen section shows granulation tissue with adjacent collagenization, gliosis and edema. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 68-year-old homeless man is found in the street and is brought to the emergency room with severe dehydration and lethargy. He is rapidly treated with intravenous fluids but lapses into coma and expires. Examination of the brainstem at autopsy shows a soft lesion in the tegmentum of the pons. A section from this region stained for myelin with luxol fast blue is shown in the image. Which of the following is associated with this disorder?

A 32-year-old woman is brought to hospital after an episode of weakness. On examination, she has decreased strength in her left upper extremity and decreased light touch sensation over her right lower extremity. She has no decrease in cognition. A lumbar puncture is performed, and the CSF contains elevated levels of IgG and oligoclonal bands on gel electrophoresis. Which of the following pathological findings in the CNS is she most likely to have?
choices: A. Gliosis with atrophy of caudate and putamen
B. Loss of pigmented neurons in the substantia nigra
C. Perivascular lymphocytes with plaques in periventricular white matter
D. Increased neurofibrillary tangles and neuritic plaques in the neocortex
E. Foci of multi-nucleated cells and macrophages in grey and white matter

question: A 32-year-old woman with insomnia undergoes an electroencephalogram (EEG) in a sleep disorder clinic. At 3:00 AM, the EEG technician notes that the women's recording was exhibiting sleep spindles and K complexes. Which stage of sleep is she in at this time?
Which of the following EEG rhythms, if recorded from an awake young adult, will be indicative of serious brain damage?
A patient is confused and displays localized jerks in his right hand, which progress to jerks of the entire arm with a brief loss of consciousness. This disorder can best be characterized as a:

A 63-year-old man has been bothered by shaking of his hand at rest and generalized stiffness of his body, which have become progressively worse during the last three years. On entering the examining room he moves slowly and deliberately, shuffling his feet, his shoulders and trunk are stooped forward and his arms at side not swinging. He has mask-like face and resting pill-rolling tremor. Neurons in which of the following areas of brain may have degenerated in this case?

A 35-year-old has an inherited progressive neuronal degeneration of brain. This resulted in twitching movement of head, grimacing movements in distal parts of upper limbs and jerking movements in distal parts of lower limbs. The neurons in which one of the following parts of the brain were degenerated in this patient?

A 30-year-old woman complains of unsteadiness while standing and walking. She tends to deviate to the right. Neurologic examination reveals dysmetria on the right side, dysdiadochokinesia, and nystagmus that is more marked when she looks to the right side. Where is the lesion located?

A 20-year-old epileptic person is brought to ER with status epilepticus. She was given intravenous lorazepam, repeated after five minutes. Her condition did not improve; therefore high dose phenytoin was given intravenously with cardiac monitoring. She did not respond well, hence intravenous phenobarbital was considered as the next step. Which one of the following would be the next logical step if phenobarbital is not effective?
Which one of the following mechanisms is not applicable to suppression of generalized tonic-clonic seizure, using the standard drug doses?
choices: A. Increased duration of opening of neuronal chloride channels
B. Increased frequency of opening of neuronal chloride channels
C. Inhibition of calcium channels in basal nuclei
D. Inhibition of neuronal glutamatergic transmission

question: A 37-year-old man who is suffering from AIDS has had increasing memory problems. During the past 3 months, he has had increasing problems with motor function and is now unable to stand or walk. MRI of the brain shows diffuse cerebral atrophy; no focal lesions are identified. On microscopic examination of the brain, which of the following findings is most likely to be present?
choices: A. Plaques of demyelination in periventricular white matter
B. Neocortical senile plaques and neurofibrillary tangles
C. Multiple lacunar infarcts in basal ganglia and gliosis
D. Pick cells (ballooned neurons), pick bodies and gliosis
E. White matter perivascular accumulation of macrophages (microglial nodules) with multinucleate cells

question: A 45-year-old woman noticed tinnitus in her right ear which progressed over 5 weeks to unilateral hearing loss. On physical examination, she has a marked decrease in hearing on the right. The other cranial nerves I-IV and IX-XII are intact. Brain MR imaging reveals a solitary, circumscribed 3 cm mass located in the region of the right cerebellopontine angle, which was resected and sent for histopathological analysis. Histologic sections from the mass would most likely reveal:
choices: A. Antoni A areas and Antoni B areas with Verocay bodies
B. A whorled pattern of meningoepithelial cells with scattered psammoma bodies
C. Endothelial proliferation and nuclear pseudopalisading of atypical astrocytic cells
D. 'Fried-egg' appearance of tumor cells with calcification
E. Small blue cells with Homer Wright rosettes

question: A 70-year-old alcoholic man has exhibited problems remembering things for the past 7 months, and he is noted by his immediate family members to confabulate. He dies as a consequence of hepatic failure. At autopsy, his brain demonstrates bilaterally small mammillary bodies revealing brown discoloration. Microscopically, there is gliosis and vascular proliferation and hemosiderin deposition in the mammillary bodies and periaqueductal grey matter. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

Several members of a large family are affected by the onset of decreasing mental function and motor coordination when they reach middle age. Their extremity movements are marked by choreoathetosis. Genetic testing reveals increased trinucleotide CAG repeats. Which of the following intracranial structures is most likely to appear grossly abnormal with radiologic imaging of these affected persons?

Clinical Scenario: A 70-year-old female was brought by her daughter to the neurology clinic with the complaints of increasing forgetfulness and progressive decline in her cognitive function. Her memory was worse than that of her contemporaries. Her daughter remarked that the patient's cognitive decline began 2 years ago when she was widowed. She remained forgetful and had difficulty with daily tasks, particularly handling money. Later she stopped attending all the social activities she had been involved in. Her family noticed she was less careful about her personal appearance and house work, about both of which she had previously been meticulous. On examination, her vitals were within normal range. She is oriented to place but not to time. She has problems copying two interlocking pentagons and her attention is normal. Her mini mental state examination score was 21/30. Routine hematology and biochemistry, serum B12, folate, and thyroid function test were normal. CT scan of head was reported as showing mild to moderate atrophy. CSF was normal except for reduced beta-amyloid levels. QUESTION: What is the most probable diagnosis in this case?
Based on the same clinical scenario (70-year-old woman with progressive cognitive decline, forgetfulness, MMSE 21/30, reduced CSF beta-amyloid): List two differential diagnoses of this case.
correctAnswer: Vascular dementia and frontotemporal atrophy (frontotemporal dementia)

question: Based on the same clinical scenario (70-year-old woman with progressive cognitive decline): According to the most probable diagnosis, what kind of pathological lesions are expected in the brain of this patient?
correctAnswer: Neurofibrillary tangles and senile plaques (neuritic plaques with amyloid beta cores)

question: Based on the same clinical scenario (70-year-old woman with progressive cognitive decline): When she was asked 'What is the capital of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia?' she replied 'Qaseem'. What kind of memory is being tested?

A 49-year-old woman presents to the ER after getting shot in the back during a robbery. On imaging, it was revealed that she suffered from a hemi-section of the spinal cord. Which of the following findings would be noted below the level of the lesion?
choices: C. Contralateral loss of crude touch

question: Which of the following pictures represents the Kernig sign?
A 15-year-old boy presents with ataxia and has a history of diabetes mellitus. Which of the following is the correct diagnosis?

A 15-year-old suffers from painful muscle cramps, weakness, and fatigue after playing soccer. After resting for a while, he was able to continue playing. After visiting his doctor, it was noted that he had elevated serum creatine kinase (CK). Which of the following is the diagnosis?

Ahmad is a psychiatrist who feels annoyed with his aggressive patient. Later on, when he thought about it he realized the patient reminded him of his ex-wife. What defense mechanism is the psychiatrist demonstrating?
Mechanism of action of methylphenidate:
choices: A. Blocks norepinephrine, serotonin, dopamine transporters equally
B. Blocks norepinephrine and serotonin transporters only
C. Blocks norepinephrine and dopamine transporters mainly
D. Blocks norepinephrine and serotonin transporters mainly

question: Which of the following drugs binds to the GABA complex increasing the chloride channel opening average time?
Which of the following has anesthetic and analgesic effects?
A depressed white 60-year-old widower presents to the psychiatric ward. Which of the following steps should be taken right away?
choices: C. Do required medical work up first
D. Reassure him and send him home

question: Trazodone is classified as which of the following drugs?
Which of the following drugs would you use for treatment-resistant depression?
A 69-year-old woman presents to the psychiatrist with trouble sleeping, eating, and feeling nervous four days before a major exam. She is worried about her symptoms. What is the next step?
choices: B. Reassure her it's normal and send her home
C. Do a blood workup to check for thyroid dysfunction
D. Tell her to stop worrying about the exam

question: Virchow-Robin space is between perforating arteries and which structure?
A baby presents with seizures after the mother presented with lesions in the genital region. Assuming that the baby was infected by a virus, what is the property of this virus?

A patient presents to the doctor to get tested for cervical cancer multiple times after their coworker at work got diagnosed years ago. Every time the patient feels a little bit of discomfort in the abdomen, they worry that it might be cancer.
A patient presents with symptoms of depression and is anxious and wants to leave the hospital. What do you do next?
choices: A. Ask why he is anxious

question: Which of the following is the correct pathway for sensitization in Aplysia?
choices: A. Binding of serotonin to Gs-protein-coupled receptor → increased cAMP → activation of PKA → phosphorylation of K+ channels
B. Binding of glutamate to NMDA receptors → increased Ca++ influx → insertion of AMPA receptors
C. Binding of serotonin to Gs-protein-coupled receptor → increased cAMP → activation of PKA → phosphorylation of transcriptional activator CREB-1
D. Activation of AMPA receptors by glutamate → increased influx of Na+ → downregulation of AMPA receptors

question: A drug that blocks T-type calcium channels leading to decreased glutamate release. Which of the following is this drug?
Serotonin decreases pain by which of the following pathway?
choices: D. Neurons from the dorsal root ganglion

question: A 69-year-old man presents to the ER with respiratory depression. He was prescribed diazepam to treat his crippling anxiety. Which of the following drugs should be administered?
One contraindication of morphine is?
Which of the following is a CSF production site?
A woman came with a severe headache into the emergency department. The ER resident suspected a subarachnoid hemorrhage. A plain CT of the head returned normal. What is the next best step?
explanation: After a negative CT, lumbar puncture to detect xanthochromia is the standard next step in suspected subarachnoid hemorrhage.

question: Which of the following types of seizures would be an indication for a boy who presented with a first-time seizure to do a CT scan?
The primary underlying problem of Parkinson's disease is:
choices: A. Unbalanced alpha and beta sheet structures
C. Increased tonic discharge from GPi
D. Multiple infarcts affecting the hippocampus

question: Formation of explicit short-term memory to long-term memory is initiated in?
A patient presents with meningitis. A lumbar puncture was performed, and CSF shows a specific picture. Which of the following investigations should be done to further identify the causative agent?
Which of the following findings in the CSF would warrant further investigation: Protein: 30 mg/100 mL | Glucose: 60 mg/100 mL | Cell count: 3 | Pressure: 260 H2O?
A woman just gave birth and started feeling slight depressive symptoms within a month of her delivery, with no effect on social activity. Which of the following is the correct diagnosis?
A patient with trouble remembering stuff that has been said to him is diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease. Which of the following drugs, if prescribed, could possibly lead to sleep disturbances?

A woman presents with inability to stay asleep with low mood, loss of interest in normal activities, increased appetite, constant fatigue. Her symptoms worsened after the birth of her 2-year-old child. Which of the following is the correct diagnosis?
Which drug inhibits the excitotoxic effects of Alzheimer's disease?
explanation: Memantine is the NMDA receptor antagonist that blocks excitotoxic glutamate activity in Alzheimer's disease. Amantadine is an antiviral/anti-Parkinson drug. The source answer of A (amantadine) appears to be an error.

question: A doctor performed an EEG on a 4-year-old child. 3 Hz spike-dome readings were found on the EEG. Which of the following is the correct diagnosis?
explanation: 3 Hz spike-and-wave complexes on EEG are the classic hallmark of absence seizures (petit mal). The source answer of E (generalized tonic-clonic) appears incorrect.

question: A 16-year-old girl with anxiety presented to a psychiatrist. Her childhood was brutal with an abusive father. Hearing the girl's story, the psychiatrist started feeling protective towards the patient in a father-like figure. What defense mechanism is demonstrated?
The Rhombic lips give rise to which of the following structures?

A patient kept complaining of symptoms of diarrhea and abdominal pain. Multiple lab investigations turned out negative. The patient had switched doctors at least 69 times. Which of the following is the correct diagnosis?

A 16-year-old girl presents to the ER with depression. She was thin and had hair-like projection from her neck (lanugo). She mentioned that she exercises very heavily and eats only 300 calories a day. Which of the following is the correct diagnosis?

A 17-year-old girl presents to the ER with unstable vitals. Her labs show metabolic alkalosis and her hands showed dorsal calluses. She mentioned that she has been on a strict diet and exercises excessively. Her BMI is 20. What is the diagnosis?
explanation: Metabolic alkalosis (from purging), dorsal hand calluses (Russell's sign from self-induced vomiting), and a normal BMI of 20 are characteristic of bulimia nervosa. Anorexia nervosa typically presents with low BMI. The source answer of A appears incorrect.

question: A patient suffering from anorexia nervosa started treatment. The resident accidentally infused too many nutrients too quickly. Which of the following is the most dangerous complication as a result of this?
Which of the following would be most concerning in a patient suffering from anorexia nervosa?
Parasympathetic fibers carried by the oculomotor nerve originate from where?

A 56-year-old patient presents to the ER with complete loss of sensation on the right side of the body. A few days later, the patient presented with freezing pain on the same side. Which of the following structures could be damaged?
A 56-year-old patient presents to the ER with complete loss of motor function on the right side of the body. He suffered from a stroke. Which of the following sites could be damaged?
choices: E. Left medial primary motor cortex

question: A patient presented to the ER after her therapist left the country. She attempted to call him at least 40 times, leaving a voice note stating that she will kill herself if he does not come back. Investigations were unremarkable for any substances or medical diseases. Which of the following is the correct diagnosis?
Which of the following is a negative symptom of schizophrenia?

A patient presented with a complaint of dizziness when turning in bed and waking up in the morning. The Dix-Hallpike maneuver showed nystagmus in less than a minute. Which of the following is the correct diagnosis?
A patient had a mutation in the VHL gene. Which of the following is the most likely finding in this patient?
A 10-year-old patient presents with ataxia. Imaging shows a mass behind the cerebellum. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 59-year-old alcoholic presents to the ER with ataxia, confusion, dysdiadochokinesia, and a tremor when performing the finger-to-nose-to-finger test. Which of the following nuclei could be affected?
explanation: Dysdiadochokinesia and intention tremor (finger-to-nose test) are signs of lateral cerebellar (neocerebellum) dysfunction, which is governed by the dentate nucleus. The fastigial nucleus governs axial/truncal coordination. The source answer B (fastigii) may be related to the gait/truncal ataxia component, but the limb signs point more to the dentate nucleus.

question: A 50-year-old man fell down the stairs on his head. Weeks later, he complained of a headache and tingling sensation in his limbs. A CT scan is shown. Which of the following is the diagnosis?
A patient suffered from a car crash. He presented to the clinic with the worst headache of his life. Hours later, he died. A CT scan before his unfortunate demise is shown. What is the diagnosis?
A 30-year-old patient presented with a headache in the morning. She was diagnosed with a tumor. Months later, the patient expired. Histology studies on autopsy are shown. What is the diagnosis?
Activation of the beta-2 receptors results in which of the following secondary messengers?

A 30-year-old office worker started experiencing headaches recently. These episodes were severe and impaired her ability to finish her tasks at work. After examination, she revealed that the headache was on the right side only. It was also revealed that the patient feels relieved when she sits in a dark room. What is the diagnosis?
Schizophrenia is considered a disease targeting:

A 60-year-old patient presents to the doctor with a complaint about being unable to feel their left face. When asked to point with their left hand at an object, they couldn't move it; however, using the right hand was possible to perform this action. Based on this presentation, the lesion is likely to be involved in which part of the brain?
choices: C. Right frontal and parietal lobe
D. Left frontal and temporal lobe

question: A 60-year-old patient presents to the doctor with a complaint about being unable to feel their left face. When asked to point with their left hand at an object, they couldn't move it; however, using the right hand was possible. Based on this presentation, a stroke would most likely be affecting which artery?
Regular use of which of the following substances may contribute to the development of amotivational syndrome, feeling stoned and more passive inward personality characteristics?
Which one of the following cellular events will occur in long-term depression (LTD)?
choices: A. More AMPA receptors will be inserted
D. N2RA subunits of NMDA receptors in the hippocampus will be bound

question: A 53-year-old man with long-standing schizophrenia has failed therapy with both first and second-generation antipsychotic agents. He still has significant problems with his mood, delusions and hallucinations. He had agranulocytosis. Which of the following drugs is he on?

A patient with communicating hydrocephalus. CT scan reveals marked enlargement of the lateral and third ventricles and a small fourth ventricle. Which of the following will be obstructed in this case?

A patient was hospitalized due to a severe car crash. Three days into his stay at the hospital, the patient became agitated, angry, and aggressive. Which of the following could be going on in this patient?
